statement.en: Architectures for Robust Self-Organizing Energy Systems under Information and Control Constraints
statement.summary: Applying the concept of controlled self-organization in agent-based Cyber-Physical Energy Systems (CPES) is a promising approach to ensure system robustness. By introducing an observer/controller architecture to the system, this concept allows for self-organization while still enabling intervention when disturbances occur. Thus, it is possible to respond to effects of cyber attacks, a major threat to current energy systems. However, when implementing an observer to monitor the system and a controller to execute actions for controlled self-organization in CPES, it is essential to take into account restrictions on information and actions resulting from the privacy of local distributed energy resources, regulatory constraints, and data exchange requirements. For this reason, this paper presen

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Paper presents provisional architecture framework for controlled self-organization in CPES. Concept is theoretically sound but implementation-dependent; claims about robustness under cyber attack constraints are not yet empirically validated (preprint status, 2026 date suggests ongoing work). The trade-off between privacy, control, and system resilience remains open and context-dependent.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 観測と制御の調和が、制約という減衰を通じて系全体の安定性へ流れ込む。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 左の眼が右の手を見守り、共通の核を守る。制約の地盤が全体を支える。
